commonIssues: Inconsistent watering causes roots to split or crack; keep soil evenly moist (RHS).
Hot, dry weather above ~24C can trigger bolting (premature flowering) and woody roots (RHS, UC IPM).
Pests and diseases: aphids, leafminers/beet leaf miner, flea beetles, webworms, Cercospora leaf spot, damping-off, root-knot nematodes (Clemson, RHS).
Internal black spot indicates boron deficiency; have soil tested before adding boron (Clemson).
Beets do not transplant well; direct-sow into the final container or bed (Illinois Extension, UC IPM).

Container minimum: choose round (globe) varieties and a pot at least ~25cm/10in deep (~11 L, ~3 gal) per Illinois Extension; RHS recommends 40cm wide and deep for best results. Beet seed is a cluster of 2-4 seeds, so thin seedlings to 3-4in apart.

source.citation: Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC 1322 'Carrot, Beet, Radish & Parsnip' — spacing, days to maturity (50-70), 5-10-10 fertilizer at planting and sidedress when plants reach 4 inches, full sun, uniform moisture to 6-inch depth, pests/diseases, boron/internal black spot, planting dates by SC region.
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC 1322 — SC planting windows (Piedmont Mar/Aug, Central Feb/Aug, Coastal Dec-Jan/Aug) and full-sun requirement.
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC 1322 — beets require full sun for optimal growth and yield; common pests, diseases and disorders.

RHS 'How to grow Beetroot' — sow late Mar/Apr-Jul; water seedlings regularly in dry spells and every 10-14 days during dry periods once established; inconsistent watering splits roots, excess water favors leaves over roots; sunny well-drained site; soil min 7C (45F) for germination; container minimum pot 40cm wide and deep with round-root varieties; bolting and woody roots in hot/dry weather; beet leaf miner, downy mildew.
RHS 'How to grow Beetroot' — container growing: minimum pot 40cm (16in) wide and deep, peat-free multi-purpose compost, choose rounded-root varieties.

UC IPM 'Cultural Tips for Growing Beets' — germination soil temp minimum 41F (5C), optimal 50-86F (10-30C); days to maturity 42-70; needs at least 4 to 6 hours of full sunlight; plants tolerate frosts and mild freezes; high temperatures cause rapid growth and white rings; frequent light irrigation when small; California planting months by region.

UC IPM 'Cultural Tips for Growing Beets' — beets tolerate frosts and mild freezes (cool-season crop); high temperatures degrade root quality, informing heatStressC ~24C as the upper comfortable limit before bolting/quality loss.

Illinois Extension 'Small-space containers big with new gardeners' / container guidance — beets and other root/leafy crops need at least ~10 inches of container depth (a 10-inch / ~3-gallon, ~11 L pot); most plants need 6-8 inches for root growth; beets direct-seeded as they do not transplant well.

The Old Farmer's Almanac 'How to Plant, Grow, and Harvest Beets' — water about 1 inch per square foot per week; cool-season crop best at ~60-65F; at least 6 hours direct sun; soil pH 6.5-7; days to maturity ~55-70.

Confidence note — coldHardyMinC set to -3C as a conservative cool-season frost-tolerance figure. Sources (UC IPM, Almanac) describe beets as cool-season crops that 'tolerate frosts and mild freezes' but give no exact lethal low temperature for the plant, so this is an estimate. heatStressC (24C) is inferred from RHS/UC IPM bolting-and-woody-root warnings in hot weather rather than a single published threshold. hotWeatherMultiplier (1.5x) and rainSkipThresholdMm (12mm) are app-tuning defaults derived from the ~1in/week and dry-spell watering guidance, not directly published numbers.
